hero.title: 5 Days Bali Bliss:
hero.intro: Five days moving through Bali's greatest hits in proper depth — Uluwatu's Kecak Fire Dance, Ubud's cultural highlands, Mount Batur's volcanic caldera, the Floating Temple at Bedugul, and Tanah Lot at sunset.

breadcrumb.label: 5 Days Bliss: Temples, Highlands & Sunset Escapes

hero.heroImageAlt: Pura Ulun Danu Beratan floating temple Bedugul Bali lake

includes: All meals as specified in itinerary
Kecak Fire Dance show at Uluwatu
BBQ seafood dinner at Jimbaran Bay
excludes: ETIQA Travel Insurance Area 2 Gold RM41

overview.heading: Five Days Exploring the Very Best of Bali
body: This five-day itinerary covers what most Bali visitors need two separate trips to see. The first day delivers the dramatic south — Uluwatu's cliffside temple, the iconic Kecak Fire Dance performed against a sunset sky, and grilled seafood at Jimbaran Bay on the sand. Day two heads into Ubud's highland circuit: silver workshops at Celuk, the Royal Palace, traditional market, Tegalalang's rice terraces, and a coffee plantation, with lunch overlooking Mount Batur's volcanic crater lake and an afternoon at Tirta Empul's sacred springs.

Day three climbs to the cool Bedugul highlands, where the Pura Ulun Danu Beratan — the Floating Temple — sits in the mist on Lake Beratan, then descends to Tanah Lot for one of Bali's most celebrated sunset photographs. Day four is completely free — a private vehicle and driver for ten hours to use however you want. Day five is a relaxed departure. It's a thorough introduction to Bali, paced to feel unhurried.

items.title: Kecak Fire Dance at Uluwatu — FREE Inclusion
items.body: Performed on a clifftop stage overlooking the Indian Ocean as the sun sets, the Kecak Dance is Bali's most theatrical cultural performance. Over a hundred bare-chested men form concentric circles, chanting the hypnotic 'cak' rhythm while dancers enact scenes from the Hindu Ramayana epic — with fire at the finale. This is included in the package at no extra charge.
items.imageAlt: Kecak Fire Dance at Uluwatu Temple Bali sunset

items.title: Pura Ulun Danu Beratan — the Floating Temple
items.body: Set on the shores of Lake Beratan in Bali's cool Bedugul highlands, the 17th-century Pura Ulun Danu appears to float on the water surface during the misty mornings when lake levels are high. Dedicated to the goddess of the lake, it is one of Bali's most photographed temples — and one of the few that genuinely looks better in person than in photos.
items.imageAlt: Pura Ulun Danu Beratan floating temple Lake Beratan Bedugul Bali

items.title: Tanah Lot Temple at Sunset
items.body: Tanah Lot — literally 'land in the sea' — is a 16th-century Hindu temple perched on a rocky islet just off Bali's west coast. At low tide it is accessible on foot; at high tide it stands alone in the surf. The temple frames one of Bali's most iconic sunset silhouettes, and the surrounding clifftop walk offers cafes and viewpoints to take it all in.
items.imageAlt: Tanah Lot sea temple Bali sunset silhouette

items.title: Ubud Highland Loop: Rice Terraces, Coffee & Mount Batur
items.body: The day-long Ubud circuit takes in Tegalalang's UNESCO-listed subak rice terraces, a working Balinese coffee plantation where you can taste kopi and luwak coffee, and lunch at a ridgeline restaurant overlooking Mount Batur's active volcano and the azure caldera lake below. Celuk village and the Ubud Royal Palace bookend the cultural side of the loop.
items.imageAlt: Tegalalang rice terrace Ubud Bali emerald terraces

items.title: Full Free Day — Private Vehicle Included
items.body: Day four is entirely yours. Your private driver and vehicle are included for up to ten hours — choose from waterfalls, beach clubs, rice terraces, temple hopping, Seminyak shopping, or simply a slow morning. Meals and entrance fees on the free day are at your own expense.
items.imageAlt: Mount Batur volcano crater lake Kintamani Bali panorama

days.title: Arrive Bali — Uluwatu, Kecak Dance & Jimbaran BBQ
days.description: Your driver-guide meets you at Ngurah Rai International Airport. The afternoon takes you to Melasti Beach — a stunning white-sand cove framed by limestone cliffs — before climbing to Uluwatu's clifftop for the Kecak Fire Dance performance at sunset. As the sky turns red, over a hundred performers chant and move on a stage that hangs above the Indian Ocean. The evening ends with a grilled BBQ seafood dinner on the sand at Jimbaran Bay. Optional: upgrade to a candlelight dinner setting with flowers and cake (RM90).
suggestedActivities: BBQ seafood dinner at Jimbaran Bay

days.title: Ubud Highlands, Mount Batur Views & Tirta Empul
days.description: A full highland day beginning with Celuk Village's gold and silver jewellery workshops, then Ubud Royal Palace and the bustling Ubud Traditional Market. Tegalalang's rice terraces offer a photogenic pause — an optional Bali Swing is available (RM68). A local coffee plantation follows, with tasting of Balinese and Luwak varieties. Lunch is at a hillside restaurant overlooking Mount Batur volcano and the volcanic caldera lake. The afternoon ends at Tirta Empul, a 10th-century spring temple where Balinese Hindus perform purification rituals. Dinner at Fork Bakery and Grill in Kuta.
suggestedActivities: Celuk Village silver and gold workshops
Tegalalang Rice Terrace (optional Bali Swing RM68)
Lunch with Mount Batur and Lake Batur views
Dinner at Fork Bakery and Grill Kuta

days.title: Bedugul Highlands, Floating Temple & Tanah Lot Sunset
days.description: The cool Bedugul highlands are the focus today. Bloom Garden showcases Bali's highland flowers in a photogenic setting before you arrive at Pura Ulun Danu Beratan, the Floating Temple on Lake Beratan — one of Bali's most distinctive heritage sites. Lunch is at Ulun Danu Resto with lake views. The afternoon includes a stop at Junglegold Chocolate Factory, where Balinese cacao is transformed into artisanal chocolate, before descending to Tanah Lot for the sunset. Optional: a one-hour Balinese full-body massage after the drive (RM90). Dinner at Tempong Indra.
suggestedActivities: Lunch at Ulun Danu Resto with lake views

days.title: Free Day — Your Private Vehicle for the Day
days.description: Today is entirely your own. A private driver and vehicle are included for up to ten hours — use them however you wish. Options include chasing waterfalls in the east, beach-clubbing in Seminyak, exploring the Nusa Dua area, visiting more temples, or shopping in Kuta. Lunch and dinner are at your own expense today.
suggestedActivities: Full day private vehicle and driver (up to 10 hours)
Suggested: Bali waterfalls, beach clubs, temple hopping, or Seminyak shopping

days.title: Leisure & Departure
days.description: A relaxed final morning. Breakfast at the hotel, free time to pick up last-minute souvenirs or enjoy the hotel pool, then a comfortable transfer to Ngurah Rai Airport in time for your flight home.

sections.heading: 5-Day Bali Package from Malaysia — Complete Temples & Highlands Tour
sections.body: This five-day itinerary is designed for Malaysian travellers who want a thorough introduction to Bali without feeling rushed. At five days, there is time to absorb the Ubud highlands properly, experience both Tanah Lot and Uluwatu at sunset, and still have an entire free day. All tours are fully private — no shared vehicles, no group schedules. The package is valid for daily departure until 31 March 2027.

sections.heading: Bali's Must-See Temples for Malaysian Travellers
sections.body: Bali has over 20,000 temples — but five stand above the rest for Malaysian visitors. This package covers three of them: Uluwatu (the clifftop sea temple where the Kecak Dance is performed), Tirta Empul (the sacred spring temple founded in 962 AD), and Pura Ulun Danu Beratan (the Floating Temple on Lake Beratan). Tanah Lot — the sea stack temple at sunset — rounds out a quartet that captures Bali's spiritual character across its most dramatic landscapes.

sections.heading: Best Time to Visit Bali from Kuala Lumpur
sections.body: Bali is a year-round destination from Malaysia, with Kuala Lumpur offering multiple direct flights daily. The dry season (April to October) sees clearer skies and is ideal for outdoor activities. July and August bring peak crowds and high season surcharges — listed separately in exclusions. The shoulder months of April–June and September–October offer excellent weather, lower hotel rates, and fewer tourists at the major temples.

items.question: Is the Kecak Fire Dance included or at extra cost?
items.answer: The Kecak Fire Dance at Uluwatu is included in this package at no extra charge — it is listed as a FREE inclusion. The performance takes place at sunset on a clifftop stage overlooking the ocean.

items.question: What is the Bloom Garden in Bedugul?
items.answer: Bloom Garden is a flower garden in Bali's cool Bedugul highlands (approximately 1,200 metres above sea level) featuring a colourful collection of exotic tropical and highland flowers. It makes for a photogenic stop before proceeding to the Floating Temple.

items.question: Can I request a specific hotel?
items.answer: Yes. The hotels listed are 'or similar' — if you have a specific preference within the same star category, contact us and we will accommodate where possible.

items.question: What happens on the free day (Day 4)?
items.answer: Day 4 comes with a private driver and vehicle for up to 10 hours. You can go anywhere in Bali — popular choices include Sekumpul Waterfall, Bali Swing, Tegallalang, Nusa Dua beach, Seminyak shopping, or a spa day. Meals and entrance fees on Day 4 are at your own expense.

items.question: Is there a minimum group size?
items.answer: No minimum group size. The package is available for 2 adults and upward on a 100% private tour basis.
